status:
Commencement date was 20 February 2019 and Completion date was 20 February 2022. The revised Completion date is 21 October 2025. Interim Completion date was 31 December 2025.

As of March 2026, cumulative Physical Progress is at 91.38% against a Revised Planned Construction Progress of 100%. Revised Time progress was 100% of the original Contract and 100% of the revised interim extension of up to 31 December 2025 has since expired. Financial Progress was 90.055% of certified IPCs and 87.47% of Paid IPCs.
Alla and Gazi bridges were completed and closed at end of Defects Notification Period. 

Aca bridge
Main box culvert structure (3-cell, 4x3m) complete.
3No. Single cell 4x3 box culverts structures complete.
1No. 2 cell 1.5x1.5 box culvert complete
3No. one cell 1.5x1.5 box culverts complete
Pioneer gravel layer in the swamp section was complete.
Swamp treatment with rockfill was complete
Contractor has halted works due to delayed payment of IPCs. Three IPCs 32, 33 and 34 are in the process of payment. The Contractor has submitted claim for extension of time (EoT) which is under review

Challenges
Delayed payment of contractor’s IPCs leading to suspension of works and delays. The Contractor temporarily suspended works due to delayed payments and works are on hold.
Rains leading to floods on site and impassable roads to the borrow pits.
Electricity poles installed during the suspension period at Aca by Ministry of Energy that may delay works if poles interfere with roadworks.

status:
Contract was signed on 4th August 2021;
Commencement date was 14 September 2021, Original Completion date was 14 September 2024, 1st Revised Completion date is 25 August 2025 and 2nd Revised Completion date is 21 November 2026.

As of March 2026, cumulative overall Physical Progress was 73.75% against Planned Progress of 100%. Time progress is 146.99% (original contract period) or 86.48% (revised contract period).
Financial Progress is 63.40% Certified (IPCs No. 1 – 9 and advance payment) or 61.69% paid (IPCs No.1 – 6 & IPC No.9) and Advance Payment)

Design
Design is complete.
Physical works on progress as below:

Nametsimeri
The structure is complete; 
Approach road works commencing.

Nabukhaya
The 10m single span bridge is complete.
Approach road works ongoing with G15 fill works in progress nearing completion.
Contractor sourcing out G30 wearing course material. Preliminary checks for bitumen ongoing for DBST.

Nambola 
The structure is complete; 
Approach road works commencing.

Khamitsaru 
10m bridge is complete.
Backfill complete.
Approach road works commencing
Final design approved.

Sahana
Bridge structure is complete.
Approach Road works resuming.

Rubongi
Bridge structure is complete;
Backfill works for bridge complete;
Clearing and grabbing for road works substantially complete
G7 and G15 fill works ongoing.

Challenges:
Lack of the Right of Way for the approach road works at Nametsimeri, Nabukhaya, Nambola, Khamitsaru and Sahana has greatly affected progress. Issue is in Court.
Delayed payment of IPCs has constrained the Contractor’s cash flow and leading to suspension of works by the Contractor. Contractor was paid outstanding dues in June 2025 and resumed normal works.
compensation: Valuation exercise was undertaken for some sections of the road. IVD exercise to be undertaken for Sahana and Nametsimeri bridges. Plans are underway for data capture for Khamitsaru bridge. PAPs have sued the GoU for illegal usage of their land.

status:
Consultancy Contract was signed on 17th  May 2024; Effective date was 10th  June 2024.

Works Contract was signed on 3rd May 2024; Commencement date was 20th June 2024.
Original completion 20 December 2025
Revised Completion 16 April 2026. (EoT 177days). 

Physical progress stands at 62% against a baseline programme that is 97.7% elapsed (679/695 days); 
Financial progress is at 60% of contract value.

Milestones so far
Revised Draft Detailed Design Report approved.
The bridge superstructure is substantially complete (piling, abutments, pier, arch ribs, deck and walkway all cast); 
current site activity is focused on road embankment earthworks (gravel end-tipping in widening sections).
All four box culverts are constructed; gabion works at Km 1+275 and diversion maintenance are planned for May 2026.
